programme.title: Hope For Wildlife
programme.desc: S7E9 - Axis Mundi: A handful of weasels are finally ready for release into the wild, a daring volunteer goes offshore to rescue an injured eagle and a gull flies free. (S7, ep 9)

programme.title: Into The Big Blue Heart
programme.desc: S1E2 - The Subtropical Zone: Embark on an incredible journey into our planet's oceans, and discover how our seas sustain not only the life within them, but all life on earth. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Wild Workers
programme.desc: S1E5 - New & Exclusive. Exterminators: Discover how nature's pest controllers keep on top of bothersome bugs, beetles and beasts and help restore balance in the natural world. (S1, ep 5)

programme.title: Wild Workers
programme.desc: S1E6 - New & Exclusive. Butchers: Get to know nature's biggest carnivores. Follow this eclectic group of creatures as they hunt and even disembowel their victims... (S1, ep 6)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: S13E15 - The great woolly monkey move around continues. Now that the bachelor group has been set up, Ayla is moved to the Pond Woollies and meets new alpha male Enzo. (S13, ep 15)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: S13E16 - Chimpanzee Kalu's health is giving cause for concern, as she's moved onto daily insulin injections to tackle her diabetes. (S13, ep 16)

programme.title: Hope For Wildlife
programme.desc: S7E10 - Party Crasher: Hope's annual open house is more popular than ever. As the whole team scrambles to get ready, an injured bobcat is rushed in for emergency care. (S7, ep 10)

programme.title: Into The Big Blue Heart
programme.desc: S1E3 - The Temperate Zone: Embark on an incredible journey into our planet's oceans, and discover how our seas sustain not only the life within them, but all life on earth. (S1, ep 3)

programme.title: Nomads Of The Serengeti
programme.desc: S1E3 - Ngorongoro: Head into the wildlife haven on the edge of the Serengeti. Formed out of a volcanic eruption, it's home to the highest concentration of mammals in the world. (S1, ep 3)

programme.title: New Kids In The Wild
programme.desc: S2E3 - Dora the Baby Muriqui: Feel the enchantment of Brazil's Atlantic Forest as southern muriqui Dora takes her first steps away from mum Mimi. (S2, ep 3)

programme.title: Deadly Australians
programme.desc: S1E2 - Wetlands: As water flows from Australia's highlands into the seas, environmental changes set the stage for unexpected clashes between predators and their prey. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Island Of The Monsoon
programme.desc: S1E6 - Life on the Wing: Yala National Park is home to 215 species of bird, that must either raise their offspring there or find a new home before the monsoon storms begin.

programme.title: Tales From Zambia
programme.desc: S1E4 - Hippo River: The Luangwa Valley is home to the world's largest concentration of hippos. But that doesn't mean they rule the river... (S1, ep 4)

programme.title: Great Parks Of Africa
programme.desc: S3E1 - The Garden Route: Land meets sea in this famous park and wildlife paradise that's home to the humpback whale, the blue duiker antelope and the endangered Knysna seahorse. (S2, ep 1)

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: S1E4 - Weird Wonders Of The Wadi: Wadis are small waterholes in the desert and are vital resources for the creatures that live there. However, danger lurks as predators join the party. (S1, ep 4)

programme.title: Wild Workers
programme.desc: S1E7 - New & Exclusive. Thieves: There are some very talented thieves in the animal kingdom. Meet the most stealthy animal scavengers on the planet as they search for a good meal. (S1, ep 7)

programme.title: Wild Workers
programme.desc: S1E8 - New & Exclusive. Diggers: There's no life without death, which is why some creatures have taken on the role of gravedigger. Discover how they make bodies disappear. (S1, ep 8)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: S13E17 - Alison receives an update on Monkey World's sister sanctuary in Vietnam. A golden-cheeked gibbon family has been released back into the wild. (S13, ep 17)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: S13E18 - Woolly monkey Pichihua becomes a member of Levar's top woollies. Xingu, the dominant female of that group, has just given birth. (S13, ep 18)

programme.title: Hope For Wildlife
programme.desc: S7E11 - Cat Out of the Bag: Hope tries to make two orphaned bobcats into playmates - with mixed results, 100 patients are ready for release and a rare bird makes a brief visit. (S7, ep 11)

programme.title: Supersenses
programme.desc: S1E1 - Humans have impressive senses, but we can only see, smell and hear a fraction of what the world has in store. The powers of animals are simply extraordinary. (1 of 3)

programme.title: Nomads Of The Serengeti
programme.desc: S1E4 - Snows of Kilimanjaro: Jean Du Plessis climbs Mount Kilimanjaro on a mission to see whether climate change is affecting snow on the top of Africa's highest free-standing peak. (S1, ep 4)

programme.title: New Kids In The Wild
programme.desc: S2E4 - Thiago the Coati Kitten: Set a course for adventure with baby coati Thiago as he sharpens his sniffing and foraging skills and learns how to cross roads and avoid danger. (S2, ep 4)

programme.title: Accidental Wilderness: Europe's Everglades
programme.desc: It was once a former wasteland where an entire city's wastewater used to be deposited. Now, it's a paradise for birds migrating from across the continent and beyond.

programme.title: After The Wildfires
programme.desc: New. A year after the tragic fire season that devastated Australia, this documentary focuses on the animals that struggled to survive, and the people who gave their all to save them.

programme.title: Great Parks Of Africa
programme.desc: S2E1 - Kruger's Pafuri: Head to a secret corner of Kruger National Park, where a single ribbon of water cuts through the parched land, creating a lifeline for creatures great and small. (S2, ep 2)

programme.title: The Invisible River- Under...
programme.desc: ...Under Water Between the Black Forest and the Vosges. Here, it's not only the the Rhine that flows, there's also a hidden, underground river where rare animals and plants thrive.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: S13E19 - There's concern as the team tries to find out what's wrong with elderly chimpanzee Kalu. (S13, ep 19)

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.desc: S7E12 - House and Home: Hope has more white-tailed deer than any previous year, and rounding them up for release is a bigger challenge than ever before! (S7, ep 12)

programme.desc: From small insects to large mammals, these different species live together, benefitting from sharing a roof.

programme.title: 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: Slovakia is up against Ukraine in Düsseldorf in Group E of EURO 2024. The most recent meeting was a 4-1 Nations League win for Slovakia in Trnava on Nov. 16, 2018. Albert Rusnák, Juraj Kucka, Adam Zrelak and Róbert Mak scored the goals for the hosts. (Soccer)

programme.title: 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: Poland plays Austria at Olympiastadion in Berlin in Group D of the 2024 European Championships. The only major tournament meeting was a 1-1 draw in Vienna in the EURO 2008 group stage. Ivica Vastic equalised with a 93rd-minute penalty for the hosts. (Soccer)

programme.title: 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: Netherlands is up against France in Leipzig in Group D of EURO 2024. Netherlands won both group stage games versus France at the EUROs (3-2 in 2000 and 4-1 in 2008), but Les Bleus eliminated Oranje on penalties in the EURO 1996 quarter-finals. (Soccer)

programme.title: The Car Knows No Difference
programme.desc: Race car driving is one of the few sports where women and men compete equally. Follow three young women who blaze a trail as they train in an elite racing program with some of the fastest factory-build racing cars. With a full sponsorship and seat on a professional team on the line, these drivers show how tactics and discipline outweigh gender roles and that the car truly knows no difference. (Special, Documentary)

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: On June 15, 2011, Vancouver hosts Game 7 of the Stanley Cup Finals between the Canucks and Boston Bruins; the city is a hive of nervous energy with the home team on the verge of its first-ever title, but when Boston stuns the Canucks to win the Cup, the situation quickly devolves into a full-blown riot, an orgy of violence that sees cars overturned and burned, windows shattered, businesses looted, and lawlessness reign... as hundreds of cell phone cameras capture it all. (Documentary)

programme.title: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith joins host Molly Qerim on this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment. (Sports talk, News)

programme.title: Golf Talk Canada
programme.desc: TSN's Mark Zecchino and Bob Weeks discuss the latest news in the world of golf. (Golf)

programme.title: NBA Today
programme.desc: In-depth, comprehensive daily coverage of all aspects of the NBA from expert analysts. (Sports talk, Basketball)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Host Laura Rutledge takes the helm of this weekday show featuring the likes of Marcus Spears, Dan Orlovsky, Mina Kimes, and Adam Schefter. The "NFL Live" crew entertains fans while offering all the latest news and analysis from across the NFL. (Football)

programme.title: Around the Horn
programme.desc: Hosted by Tony Reali in the ESPN studio and joined via satellite by four panelists from across the country, the team debates popular sports stories. Reali acts as moderator, giving points for well-made arguments and deducting points for those who inspire him to hit the "mute" button. At the end of the show, whomever Reali deems the winner has 30 seconds without interruption to voice their final opinions on any topic they want. (Sports talk)

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day. (Sports talk)

programme.title: SportsCentre
programme.desc: TSN's flagship show providing news, highlights, analysis and interviews from the world of sports. (News, Sports talk)

programme.title: MLS Soccer
programme.desc: The Red Bulls make their first trip north of the border this year when they visit CF Montréal. The clubs traded wins in their last eight meetings, with both taking a victory in each of the last three seasons. Montréal is unbeaten in four straight. (Soccer)

programme.desc: Hosted by Tony Reali in the ESPN studio and joined via satellite by four panelists from across the country, the team debates popular sports stories. Reali acts as moderator, giving points for well-made arguments and deducting points for those who inspire him to hit the "mute" button. At the end of the show, whomever Reali deems the winner has 30 seconds without interruption to voice their final opinions on any topic they want. (Sports talk)

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day. (Sports talk)

programme.title: Super Agent Jon le Bon
programme.desc: Whitewash sends a package to the Agency that only his seven hidden rock cousins can disable. (Repeat).

programme.title: Future History
programme.desc: Kris gets a personal tour by brother and sister scholars Jade and Mitch Huguenin who are largely responsible for the inclusion of Metis history and knowledge in the historical site of Discovery Harbour in Penetanguishene. (Repeat). (Documentary)

programme.title: Future History
programme.desc: Kris explores the Sheguiandah Quarry on Manitoulin Island. Sarah meets Tasheena Sarazin. (Repeat). (Documentary)

programme.title: BC Today
programme.desc: (New).

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A globetrotting couple have decided to move back home for good, and Nicki Chapman lends a hand as they try to find a new address in Oxfordshire's lovely countryside for their £650k budget. Nicki also discovers how and why the county is leading the way with the recycling of its food waste. (Repeat). (Reality)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: DS Swain visits Roy to ask for the true cause of his and Carla's estrangement. (New). (Drama, Soap)

programme.title: Bears' Lair
programme.desc: Four finalists bring their A-game with new, improved pitches for the Bears and four guest judges. Then, one lucky entrepreneur walks away with the grand prize of $100,000. (New).

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch investigates when a Japanese spy is found dead in a Korean family's cellar. (Repeat). (Drama, Science)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: An expectant mother buckles under the strains of pregnancy. As Tom provides pastoral care, he reveals why this case has touched him more than most. (Repeat). (Drama)

programme.title: The Great Canadian Baking Show
programme.desc: The four remaining bakers attempt to pass three pie challenges. (Repeat). (Reality, Food & Cooking)

programme.title: CBC Vancouver News
programme.desc: Comprehensive coverage of Vancouver events, sports, and weather. (New).

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark falls under the spell of a visiting hypnotist and lets his grades slip. Pop struggles to lose weight when he gets too fat for his pants. (Repeat). (Comedy)

programme.title: Just for Laughs: Gags
programme.desc: A hidden-camera show modeled after the popular series "Just for Laughs." (Repeat).

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A confectionery couple give the Dragons a taste of history; a cheerful young entrepreneur hopes her new app will get the Dragons jumping for joy; and a mustachioed inventor has a close shave in the Den. Also: Siblings have a product they hope the Dragons won't roll over on. (Repeat). (Reality, Business)

programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: It's 1996. Business executive Jim Balsillie and computer genius Mike Lazaridis form an unlikely partnership in an effort to bring the world's first smartphone to the mobile market. (Repeat). (Comedy, Tech & Gaming)

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship newscast, featuring depth and context from the CBC's bureaus across the country and around the world. (New).

programme.title: CBC Vancouver News
programme.desc: Comprehensive coverage of Vancouver events, sports, and weather. (New).

programme.title: Canadian Reflections
programme.desc: (Repeat). (Arts)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: DS Swain visits Roy to ask for the true cause of his and Carla's estrangement. (Repeat). (Drama, Soap)

programme.title: Arctic Vets
programme.desc: The vets check up on endangered animals in their care. Bijou, an aging lynx, is behaving abnormally because of a potential kidney failure. (Repeat). (Reality)

programme.title: Arctic Vets
programme.desc: Dr. Chris heads to the Cochrane Polar Bear Habitat to treat the injured leg of a polar bear. (Repeat). (Reality)

programme.title: Skindigenous
programme.desc: The Paiwan people are one of about 20 Indigenous minorities of Taiwan. Cudjuy Patjidres has developed his artistic skills from watching his grandfather weave and carve wood. He is now dedicated to preserving the ancient symbols and designs. (Repeat). (Documentary)

programme.title: Skindigenous
programme.desc: Audie Murray works with traditional Cree tattoos and Michif visual culture. (Repeat). (Documentary)

programme.title: Lac-Mégantic: This Is Not an Accident
programme.desc: As downtown burns, citizens search for the missing. The scope of the destruction is evident, but the cause is unknown and no one takes responsibility for what's happened. (Repeat).

programme.title: Strays
programme.desc: Shannon makes Hugh and Liam an offer that calls her relationship with Jeffrey into question. Nikki is closely connected to her new friend, and Joy helps Paul send a care package to his daughter. (Repeat). (Comedy)

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship newscast, featuring depth and context from the CBC's bureaus across the country and around the world. (Repeat).

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A desire to move closer to family fuels buyers to move from Birmingham to Lincolnshire. (Repeat). (Reality)

programme.title: The Early Edition
programme.desc: Morning newsmagazine. (New).

programme.title: Mighty Express
programme.desc: Faye and Nico must deliver baby skunks back to the forest, but they get into lots of stinky situations along the way. (Repeat).

programme.title: Mini-Jon and Mini-Maple
programme.desc: Mini-Jon must pass a very, very difficult physical test, but there's always a way to cheat. (Repeat). (Comedy)

programme.title: Kingdom Force
programme.desc: Max Volume subjects all the badgers in the Canyon Kingdom under mind control with a Hypno-speaker. The Kingdom Force forms the Alpha-Mech and was able to destroy parts of the machine but not the speakers, enabling Max Volume to hypnotize TJ to go against his team. (Repeat). (Comedy, Action & Adventure, Kids)

programme.title: Dino Ranch
programme.desc: The Tinhorns and the Cassidys face off in a race, but it is only through learning to work together that they are able to finish the race. (Repeat). (Family, Comedy, Action & Adventure)

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: A fun day on Jake's mountain for Katie and her cat turns a little bit scary when they get stuck in a ski lift, but lucky for them, Ryder and the Paw Patrol are coming to their rescue. (Repeat). (Comedy, Action & Adventure, Kids)

programme.title: Hello Kitty: Super Style!
programme.desc: When Kitty discovers Pinky is having a blue day, she goes on a mission to cheer her up. (Repeat). (Comedy)

programme.title: Jeremy and Jazzy
programme.desc: Two of Jeremy's friends have very confusing names, and it causes them all sorts of trouble. (Repeat). (Kids)

programme.title: Aunty B's House
programme.desc: Zach is overjoyed when Simon arrives for a sleepover at Aunty B's place.This is Simon's first sleepover, and Aunty B's House has a lot of unusual shadows and sounds. Aunty assists him. (Repeat).

programme.title: Big Blue
programme.desc: Lemo is thrilled when he is called to join United Current Head Quarters and asked to apply his fun brand of science to headquarters staff. (Repeat).

programme.title: Rusty Rivets
programme.desc: Rusty saves a remote-controlled plane. (Repeat). (Family, Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.desc: A young Paddington writes letters to his aunt Lucy, celebrating the new things he has discovered through the day's exciting activities. (Repeat). (Family, Kids)

programme.title: Let's Go, Bananas!
programme.desc: On a day out at Ladybug World, Apple feels like the world's lonely only child. So she starts playing brothers and sisters with allher friends in search of the ideal sibling. (New).

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: Auntie Midge's excitement for the Spring Carnival is dampened when she injured her hip. (Repeat). (Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: Tooey needs help to fix a hole in his Christmas sweater, knit by his grandma who is about to arrive for the holidays. (Repeat). (Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: A family of beavers builds a dam and accidentally diverts water into Trini's garden. The kids must devise a way to redirect the stream before Trini's strawberries are ruined. (Repeat). (Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: Trini works hard to earn the Night Watchers Badge. (Repeat). (Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: Molly trains so she can participate in a cross-country ski race where Sharon Firth will speak. (Repeat). (Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: When Molly and her family go fly fishing, a seal sneaks into their boat and eats their lunch. (Repeat). (Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: Tooey is worried one of his sled dogs, Cali, isn't feeling well. When Tooey, Molly and Trini take her to a vet for a check-up, they receive some surprising news. (Repeat). (Comedy, Educational, Kids)

programme.title: Future History
programme.desc: Kris goes to Ottawa to meet Elaine Kicknosway, 60's Scoop Advocate and Co-Founder of The National Indigenous Survivors of Child Welfare Network. (Repeat). (Documentary)

programme.title: The Elders
programme.desc: Mr. Cao retired. Mr. Meng owns a pet shop. The two argued as Mr. Cao suspected that the pet bird he bought from Mr. Meng was ill. Meanwhile the most precious pet of the shop, a parrot called Xiaoqing, was missing. Mr. Cao decided to help Mr. Meng. (Repeat). (Drama)

programme.title: BC Today
programme.desc: (New).

programme.title: Absolutely Canadian
programme.desc: Canadian documentaries, concerts and comedy performances are featured. (Repeat).

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy is drawn from the safety of his cell to find a vengeful Griff waiting for his return. (New). (Drama, Soap)

programme.desc: Germany is up against Hungary in Stuttgart in Group A of the 2024 European Championships. The only EUROs meeting was a 2-2 group stage draw in Munich in June 2021. Leon Goretzka's 84th-minute equaliser put the Germans through to the knockout phase. (Soccer)

programme.title: 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: Slovakia is up against Ukraine in Düsseldorf in Group E of EURO 2024. The most recent meeting was a 4-1 Nations League win for Slovakia in Trnava on Nov. 16, 2018. Albert Rusnák, Juraj Kucka, Adam Zrelak and Róbert Mak scored the goals for the hosts. (Soccer)

programme.desc: The latest news and analyses from the UEFA European Championship. (Soccer)

programme.title: 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: Poland plays Austria at Olympiastadion in Berlin in Group D of the 2024 European Championships. The only major tournament meeting was a 1-1 draw in Vienna in the EURO 2008 group stage. Ivica Vastic equalised with a 93rd-minute penalty for the hosts. (Soccer)

programme.desc: The latest news and analyses from the UEFA European Championship. (Soccer)

programme.title: 2024 UEFA European Championship
programme.desc: Netherlands is up against France in Leipzig in Group D of EURO 2024. Netherlands won both group stage games versus France at the EUROs (3-2 in 2000 and 4-1 in 2008), but Les Bleus eliminated Oranje on penalties in the EURO 1996 quarter-finals. (Soccer)

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.desc: S4E1 - Daniel learns he can have fun on his own, with creativity and imagination; Daniel and his friends make up silly games to play. (Animated, Children, Educational)

programme.title: Caillou
programme.desc: Leo and Clementine are playing in the sandbox, and Caillou accidentally breaks a part of the sand castle as well as Clementine's bucket; Caillou is very worried when Leo has to go to the hospital. (Animated, Children, Educational)

programme.desc: S1E17 - Chip has her first sleepover. (Animated, Children, Comedy drama)

programme.title: Lucas the Spider
programme.desc: S1E13 - After seeing the perfect web in the window of the house, Lucas questions his spider-hood when he realizes he can't spin one properly. (Animated, Children, Fantasy)

programme.title: Lucas the Spider

programme.desc: S3E12 - Daniel and Grandpere hunt for buried treasure; Grandpere takes Daniel on a sunrise boat ride before the family heads back home. (Animated, Children, Educational)

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.desc: S3E4 - Daniel and Katerina have to make their own noisemakers when Margaret wants her rattle back; Daniel, Prince Wednesday and Miss Elaina create a pretend neighborhood. (Animated, Children, Educational)

programme.title: Caillou
programme.desc: Caillou helps Grandma put together a belated surprise party for Grandpa's birthday; Caillou doesn't want to sleep in his own bed after he has a nightmare. (Animated, Children, Educational)

programme.desc: S1E56 - The Super Simple Puppets sing "Skidamarink," a sweet nursery rhyme. (Children, Music)

programme.title: Super Simple Songs
programme.desc: S1E49 - Sing along with this super simple bath song for kids! The baby in the bath goes wash. wash, wash. Wash, wash, wash. Wash, wash, wash. The baby in the bath goes wash, wash, wash. Let's take a bath. (Children, Music)

programme.title: Super Simple Songs
programme.desc: S1E58 - "The Bees Go Buzzing" is a new take on the classic kids song, "The Ants Go Marching"; these bumblebees make their way back to their hive to the song. (Children, Music)

programme.title: Madagascar: A Little Wild

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A man swallowed whole by a fish, a body eaten alive from within. Another penetrated in a most unnatural fashion, yet more with multiple stab wounds. Jeremy Wade is in pursuit of some of the Amazon's most notorious flesh eaters.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: No fish inspires the same terror as the shark. A creature with an insatiable hunger for brutal violence. But at least these killers are confined to the oceans. Or are they? Expert angler Jeremy Wade investigates the deadly Bullshark.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to The Mekong River in Thailand in search of one of the world's largest, most terrifying river fish, the Giant Freshwater Stingray. Armed with a venomous 10-inch barbed tail, this 700-pound monster tests Jeremy to his physical limit.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade travels to one of the few rivers left on earth that will still test a fisherman. The Congo, a river steeped in legends, and soaked in violence. One spirit, in particular, is said to lure fishermen from their boats to their deaths.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Tom's run of bad luck intensifies, but he refuses to give up; Morgan closes in on his goal and Marty prepares for his family's arrival on the line.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Morgan's survival hangs on a critical airdrop; gale-force winds threaten to destroy Marty's cabin; Tom finally gets a breakthrough that could end his losing streak.

programme.title: Black Gold
programme.desc: Big Dog's Driller Justin is losing patience with Derek, the rig's worm. Josh and the young Longhorn crew drill into poison gas. Oilman Mike LaMonica rushes to the Viking rig when Wayne's crew faces a crisis that could cost them the well.

programme.title: Black Gold
programme.desc: On Longhorn, Driller Josh's unsafe performance puts his crew and his job in jeopardy. Tensions between Big Dog's Justin and Derek finally come to a head. Wayne's Viking rig makes an unexpected find and Mike may have discovered Black Gold.

programme.title: Black Gold
programme.desc: Big Dog's owner, Autry Stevens, races to save 4 expiring leases that will be worth half a billion dollars in oil production.

programme.title: Yukon Men
programme.desc: The rivers around Tanana are dangerously melting. Most hunters have made the deadly journey back home but Charlie's brother-in-law, George, is missing. Search & rescue efforts are unsuccessful. A town of 200 people has to face a shocking reality.

programme.title: Yukon Men
programme.desc: It's finally Spring in Tanana and Joey Zuray is ready to move out on his own. The only thing he has to do is move the house he wants across town before it gets demolished in a few days. Charlie hunts a deadly black bear that's menacing the village.

programme.title: Yukon Men
programme.desc: It's driftwood logging season, and there's a brief window to pull firewood out of local rivers. James relies on his young son to help make ends meet. Stan gets caught in a powerful storm and Charlie battles a deadly black bear.

programme.title: Dual Survival
programme.desc: Caught in the jungle, the guys face an injury that needs medical attention.

programme.title: Dual Survival
programme.desc: Joe and Matt confront the dangers of the border between Texas and Mexico.

programme.title: Dual Survival
programme.desc: Joe and Matt battle dehydration and altitude in the Bolivian Andes.

programme.title: Man vs. Wild: Vietnam
programme.desc: Deep in the heart of Vietnam, Bear Grylls encounters blood-sucking leeches, a deadly Spitting Cobra, eats a Forest Scorpion and avoids a violent mountain river flood as he tangles with the worst the jungle can throw at him.

programme.title: Man vs. Wild: Texas
programme.desc: In Western Texas, Bear falls to earth from an upturned bi-plane, to lock horns with the parched Chihuahuan Desert; he traverses deep gorges, locates an oasis and takes on a diamondback rattlesnake.

programme.title: Man vs. Wild: Alaska
programme.desc: Bear Grylls is in the wilds of Alaska where he ice-hops onto a glacier, is left hanging by his fingertips over a forest waterfall and narrowly avoids getting crushed between an iceberg and his rescue vessel.

programme.title: Man vs. Wild: The Inside Story
programme.desc: The Man vs. Wild crew who follow Bear Grylls on his travels across the globe, tell how it feels to follow in his footsteps and reveal just how they make Man vs. Wild.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Morgan is sidelined while he gets back on his feet; Marty fights to stay in the game and Eustace makes a critical trade to protect his future.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Marty doubles down on a new strategy to keep from losing more ground; Morgan and Margaret defend what's theirs and Jason's hunt for land becomes an ordeal.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Marty fights for every last dollar as his season goes into freefall; Eustace and Preston experiment in barnyard engineering and Jason gets locked out of a new home.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: An early thaw puts Morgan's supply drop on thin ice; Marty hunts the beasts of Kodiak Island and Tom makes the most of his unprecedented winning streak.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Morgan slips up navigating the meltdown that's transforming the Alaskan wild; Marty's high altitude hunt takes him to the top of Kodiak Island's most treacherous peaks

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler, Jeremy Wade, searches for the lethal lake monster of native legend that's been dragging people down to an icy grave for centuries. Jeremy ends up landing the largest fish he's ever caught, but is this the monster?

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade is on a quest to Africa's Rift Valley, to explore the roots of fishing and the monsters these first fishermen faced. A dangerous place, with killer crocs, hippos and warring gangs, but at its heart is a worthy prize: the Mputa, Aka Nile Perch.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade journeys to the infamous Congo River in Africa in search of the world's most ferocious fish. This supernatural monster predator pushes Jeremy to his physical and mental limits. It requires all of his skills and the help of a witch doctor.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade is on the trail of a mysterious man-eater described over two hundred years ago by a famous explorer. Is the eight-foot beast in question a mere legend, or could it be the same, flesh and blood creature that has attacked divers and children?

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: In Alaska, Marty Meierotto completes his cabin build just in time for his family to touch down in the Revelation Mountains.

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: A breakdown leaves Marty stranded after dark; Eustace must improvise when he comes up shorthanded.

programme.title: Black Gold
programme.desc: Gerald's finally working on Big Dog 28, but he's working as a derrickman. A rookie mistake risks everyone's jobs.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Maritime Museum, London
programme.desc: From the musket shot that killed Admiral Nelson to an anti-piracy commando raid, discover the secrets inside the National Maritime Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Topkapi Palace Museum, Istanbul
programme.desc: From assassins who plot to poison a Sultan to bitter enemies who sign the world’s first peace treaty, discover the secrets inside the Topkapi Palace Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Museum of Anthropology, Mexico City
programme.desc: From ancient athletes who kick a severed head to a peasant dress that brings down an empire, discover the secrets inside the National Museum of Anthropology.

programme.title: Victorian Farm
programme.desc: The team’s year on the Victorian Farm is coming to an end. But first they have to bring in the Wheat Harvest, the most crucial part of the Victorian Farm calender.

programme.title: Victorian Farm
programme.desc: The team move into a Victorian smallholding on the Acton Scott estate that hasn’t been used in nearly half a century. Their first task is the restoration of the cottage – a task that threatens to get the better of them.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: The Gold Rush
programme.desc: In a saw mill belonging to Swiss pioneer John Sutter, James W. Marshall, a carpenter finds pieces of shining metal in the stream next to the saw mill: it’s gold!

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Adrianople: Rome Against the Barbarians
programme.desc: A stroke is at the origin of the destruction of the most powerful empire ever known.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Miners: Fodder of the Industrial Revolution
programme.desc: The story of coal, inseparable from that of the Industrial Revolution, and of social protest, the rise of socialism, anarchism and trades unions.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Einstein and the Manhattan Project
programme.desc: Albert Einstein, a confirmed pacifist, flees Nazi Germany and takes refuge in the United States.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Gutenberg and the Printing Press
programme.desc: 1450. Mayence. Johannes Gutenberg has been working on creating a printing press for more than 2 years.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Encyclopedia: Dare to Know
programme.desc: The famous French philosopher, whose work is a pillar of modern thought, opens the way to the Age of Enlightenment.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Diên Biên Phu: a Losing Battle
programme.desc: On February 03 1954 the siege of the entrenched Dien Bien Phu camp begins. France loses its colonial empire to the Vietnamese communists.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Chernobyl, a Reactor Out of Control
programme.desc: The Chernobyl disaster is the most serious ever recorded on our planet: a nuclear accident that is 7 on a scale of 7.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Confucius and Chinese Thought
programme.desc: The struggle for power has rendered the situation unstable and the ancient Zhou dynasty has lost its role as unifier and peace-broker.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Darwin Discovering the World
programme.desc: The shape of Darwin’s nose almost prevented one of the greatest revolutions in the history of science.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Aral, a Sea for Cotton
programme.desc: In the 1960s, the Aral Sea, lying astride Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan, is the fourth largest lake in the world.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Chateau of Versailles, Paris
programme.desc: From the schemes of Marie Antoinette to the spectrum of a star that reveals the origin of human life, discover the secrets inside the Chateau of Versailles.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the American Museum of Natural History, New York
programme.desc: From a dinosaur embryo that solves a murder mystery to a spacecraft catching a comet’s tail, discover the secrets inside the American Museum of Natural History.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Louvre
programme.desc: From murdered kings to stolen masterpieces, discover the secrets inside the Louvre.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Uffizi Gallery, Florence
programme.desc: From a famous artist who vanished to a traitorous Nazi who saved the day, discover the secrets inside the Uffizi Gallery.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Metropolitan Museum, New York
programme.desc: From the mystery of a king’s madness to a silver chalice some think is the Holy Grail, discover the secrets inside the Metropolitan Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the State Hermitage Museum
programme.desc: From ancient mummies who sport tattoos to re-examining who really killed Rasputin, discover the secrets inside the State Hermitage Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Pergamon and Neues Museums, Berlin
programme.desc: From Vikings who break unbreakable swords to curators who solve the world’s hardest jigsaw puzzle, discover the secrets inside the Pergamon and Neues Museums.

programme.title: M.A.D. World: Peace and War
programme.desc: 1970s: US withdraws from Vietnam. Neutron bomb tested, arms race escalates. Civil war erupts in Angola, mercenaries and weapons flood in. Bright spot: Carter, Brezhnev sign nuclear arms limitation treaty in 1979.

programme.title: M.A.D. World: Cold War 2
programme.desc: Cold War II escalates as Soviet military enters Afghanistan, sparking Western outrage. US supports Mujahedeen rebels. Muhammad Ali leads Olympic boycott in 1980. 'No Nukes' protests erupt amid fear of global annihilation.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Angels of Death
programme.desc: In hospitals and nursing wards, death is an everyday occurrence. And that is precisely what they count on: angels of death. Some of them rack up more victims in this way than the worst serial killers in the world.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Rampage Killers
programme.desc: Rampage Killers. They come out of nowhere – and when they occur, innocent people always die. Investigators must react in a flash and make difficult decisions. Every killing spree is a race against time.

programme.title: Murder Maps: Terror In The Roaring Twenties
programme.desc: Edith Thompson and Frederick Bywaters were in love. But the young couple were convicted for murdering her husband in a case that shocked society and fascinated Alfred Hitchcoc

programme.title: Murder Maps: The Blackout Ripper

programme.desc: From 845 onwards, murderous raids and destructive sieges carried out by the Scandinavians accelerated the breakup of the Carolingian Empire.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Lawrence of Arabia: For a Fistful of Sand
programme.desc: The story of the famous archeologist, agent for the British, who, during the First World War, took part in the Arab uprising against the Ottoman Turks.

programme.title: Robson's Extreme Fishing Challenge: Episode 1
programme.desc: Robson Green travels the World challenging the best fisherman to catch the biggest, best fish across the planet.

programme.title: Robson's Extreme Fishing Challenge: Episode 3
programme.desc: Robson Green travels the World challenging the best fisherman to catch the biggest, best fish across the planet.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Giant Sturgeon
programme.desc: GIANT STURGEON. Famed Canadian outdoorsman Helgy Eymundson searches for the 1,000lb Sturgeon of the Fraser River.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Prince Edward Island Tuna
programme.desc: MONSTER BLUEFIN TUNA. Renowned stand up fisherman, Fred Lavitman, returns to Prince Edward Island to beat his own record. He breaks down the tactics, risks, gear, and techniques to land one of these 1,000lb plus giants.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Catfish of France
programme.desc: BIRD-EATING CATFISH. Snatching birds from the air, these monster catfish have convinced Fred to travel overseas to fish this special spot in France.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Canadian Sturgeon
programme.desc: MONSTER STURGEON up to a thousand pounds on the upper Fraser River, British Columbia. Helgy Eymundson will try to land the fish of a lifetime.

programme.title: Monster Fish: The Canyons
programme.desc: THE CANYONS. Fred Lavitman and crew fish through the night in search of massive Tuna and record numbers of Swordfish.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Northumberland Strait Bluefin
programme.desc: 1,000 LB+ BLUEFIN TUNA. Fred battles monster Bluefin in the Northumberland Strait in Canadian waters.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Marlin vs. Permit
programme.desc: MARLIN VERSUS PERMIT. Trev Gowdy hunts down massive permit in the Florida Keys, while Fred Lavitman targets monster Marlin off the Bahamian coast.

programme.title: Tuna of Scotland
programme.desc: BIG DISCOVERY OF SCOTTISH TUNA. Join Fred Lavitman as he travels to Scotland, sees the sights, and fights to land a monster Tuna in these rough waters.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Season Countdown
programme.desc: GIANT TREVALLY in Australia, MASSIVE TUNA, HUGE WAHOO, and MAMMOTH STURGEON. These are the monster fish of the year.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Striped Bass versus Mississippi Kingfish
programme.desc: STRIPED BASS VERSUS KINGFISH. Fred fishes off the Louisiana coast for huge Kingfish. Meanwhile Trev tests new baits in search of 50lb Stripers.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Giant Sturgeon versus Monster Swordfish
programme.desc: GIANT STURGEON VERSUS MONSTER SWORDFISH. Fred Lavitman battles Giant Sturgeon in British Columbia, while Al McGlashan lands monster Swordfish off the southern coast of Australia.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Alaskan Rainbow Trout/Alligator Gar
programme.desc: ALASKAN RAINBOW TROUT/ALLIGATOR GAR. Trev Gowdy hunts down massive Rainbows in Alaska, while Fred targets monster Alligator Gar in this murky, Texas river.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Tigerfish of Tanzania
programme.desc: MONSTER TIGERFISH of Tanzania. Head guides Stu Harley and Greg Ghaui of African Waters explore the untouched river systems of Tanzania in search of vicious Tigerfish with a wicked bite.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Bass Fishermen versus Largemouth
programme.desc: MONSTER LARGEMOUTH BASS. Professional Anglers Kelly Jordon and Ott DeFoe are on the hunt for monster Largemouth Bass. Which area holds the biggest giants, Texas or Florida?

programme.title: Monster Fish: Islamorada, Florida Tiger Shark
programme.desc: GIANT TIGER SHARKS attack massive schools of amberjack 40 miles off the coast of Florida. Fred fights his way up the food chain to bring a true monster Tiger Shark to the boat.

programme.title: Monster Fish: African Tigerfish
programme.desc: MASSIVE AFRICAN TIGERFISH. Head guides Stu Harley and Greg Ghaui of African Waters hunt for the record-breaking Tigerfish in the remote, murky waters of Tanzania.

programme.desc: Robson Green travels the World challenging the best fisherman to catch the biggest, best fish across the planet.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Hunting Tuna
programme.desc: Armed with only a hand-thrown harpoon, these monster-hunters look to bring in giant Tuna using the old ways.

programme.title: Monster Fish: Gabon Africa Bull Sharks
programme.desc: Waves crash onto the remote African shoreline. Armed with nothing more than rod and reel, angler Mark Murray wades into the deep to hunt one of the most dangerous sharks on the planet, the Bull Shark.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Tornadoes
programme.desc: The Tornado is one of Mother Nature’s most extraordinary phenomena. These intense, rapidly rotating columns of air can attain wind speeds of more than 480 kilometres per hour, and have been witnessed on every continent except Antarctica.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Avalanches
programme.desc: We tend to think of disasters as inevitable - but often Mother Nature gives us vital clues which we foolishly ignore or overlook. This episode will take a close look at avalanches and what we can learn about how, where, and why they strike.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Mosquitoes
programme.desc: In 2014, Microsoft Founder Bill Gates described the mosquito as the “deadliest animal in the world.” It is estimated by the World Health Organisation that they kill roughly 750,000 people per year.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Earthquakes
programme.desc: Many of our most densely populated cities are situated on the Earth’s most seismically active fault lines. Deadly, highly destructive, and impossible to predict, Earthquakes are truly one of Mother Nature’s most terrifying forces.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Antarctica
programme.desc: From "Fin Del Mundo," George sails on a Russian icebreaker to Antarctica. After braving stormy Drake passage, he explores volcanic Deception Island, camps in Antarctic snow and paddles a kayak through icebergs calved off melting glaciers.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hawaii: Surf & Snow
programme.desc: In Hawaii George explores the wild side: diving with Galapagos sharks, ascending to snow-covered Mauna Kea, dental cleaning by Peppermint Cleaner Shrimp, extreme surfing at Banzai Pipeline, getting his boots lit on fire by Kilauea volcano.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Elephant Cave
programme.desc: Waterfall-shrouded Kitum Cave has been carved out by elephants in search of salt on Mount Elgon at the Kenya-Uganda border. George gets bitten by one of the cave bats suspected of carrying the deadly Marburg virus.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hottest and Coldest
programme.desc: George meets ultra-marathon runners in searing heat of Death Valley and bone-chilling cold of the Yukon. He dog-sleds in snow and meets wild characters like Caveman Bill, who has lived alone in a cave through 12 long cold Yukon winters.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Indonesia
programme.desc: In Indonesia George visits massive Komodo Dragons, explores steaming sulfur vents of Papandayan, visits villagers whose town got swept away by Sidoarjo Mudflow, and tackles his toughest climb to date - the very active Semeru Volcano.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Timbuktu: Birthplace of Hurricanes
programme.desc: Dust storms can spin off from Africa into the Atlantic and morph into tropical hurricanes that threaten the Caribbean and USA. While searching for Timbuktu's dust storms, George finds adventure in the landscape and culture of West Africa.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Wild Weather Season
programme.desc: 2005 seemed to be the year that Earth went wild. Follow George as he brings us back to the site of his hairiest adventures from that year.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Fire And Water
programme.desc: It's the intense summer wildfires that often plague the area during their 24 hours of daylight. He travels by train, bike, plane and whitewater raft to reach the fires but Mother Nature has other plans for him and doesn't make things easy.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Volcanic Wedding
programme.desc: Most people's idea of a dream wedding might include a horse drawn carriage and an elaborate ceremony. Not George. Join him as he and his bride to be tie the knot in an unusual and certainly exotic way.

programme.title: Naked Science: Atmosphere
programme.desc: We breathe it every day, it protects us from solar radiation, controls our weather – it’s the atmosphere. Naked Science takes a trip from the earth to near space to understand how the earth’s atmosphere affects all life on the planet.

programme.title: Naked Science: Great Lakes
programme.desc: The Great Lakes are sometimes referred to as inland seas; they are the largest freshwater system in the world. Naked Science examines the early history of these amazing bodies

programme.title: Naked Science: Mars
programme.desc: Mars our closest neighbor now a lifeless planet – but it may once have had water and therefore life. Naked Science investigates the red planet to learn how it was formed, how it became barren, and ask whether it ever will be possible to colonize it?

programme.title: Naked Science: Death Of The Earth
programme.desc: The earth is 4.5 billion years old and it is slowly dying. Tectonic activity is slowly cooling the planet – the continents will eventually move together to form a new supercontinent called Pangea Ultima.

programme.title: Naked Science: Jupiter
programme.desc: By far the largest planet, 1300 times the size of Earth, it’s a world so enormous it could swallow every planet and moon in the solar system and still have room to spare.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Heatwaves
programme.desc: Temperatures in places like Saudi Arabia routinely exceed 40 degrees Celsius with very little effect on the population. However, when the same extreme heat struck the people of Russia in 2010, it killed an estimated 56,000 people.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Landslides
programme.desc: Nothing can be done to prevent a landslide once it starts; they are among the most ferocious and devastating forces in nature.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Famine
programme.desc: Every continent on Earth has experienced this terrifying phenomenon at some point in its history. It’s ravaged the world for centuries, as crop infestation, drought, flooding and disease have killed thousands.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Floods
programme.desc: Water may seem harmless, but in enormous quantities, it can be an astonishingly destructive force. Apart from famine, floods have resulted in more deaths worldwide than any other natural disaster.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Volcanoes
programme.desc: Human history is littered with the stories of gigantic volcanic eruptions and the tragic fates of those who could not outrun the burning lava and poisonous gases. In our own times, volcanoes continue to cause large-scale destruction.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Italia Wild
programme.desc: In Italy, George studies avalanches of Dolomite Mountains, meets engineers who keep Venice from sinking, and heads South to explore Italy's explosive volcanoes: Vesuvius, Etna and Stromboli.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Crystal Cave
programme.desc: In 2000 in Chihuahua, Mexico, miners found a cave filled with giant crystals. Temperatures reach 120 degrees F and humidity nearly 100%. George leads an Explorers Club Flag Expedition into this extreme environment.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Monsoon
programme.desc: In India's monsoon season George wades through waist-deep water in Bombay and leach-filled waterfalls in Goa, finds people being swept away by rivers, and rebuilding bridges. He experiences an intense annual Hindu celebration.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hurricane Triple Threat
programme.desc: George chases deadly hurricanes in the USA: Gustav in Louisiana and Hanna in North Carolina. He then flies with USAF Hurricane Hunters into Ike over Cuba, meeting it again as it makes a wild destructive landfall in Galveston, Texas.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Tornadoes
programme.desc: George embarks on his annual pilgrimage to the "Tornado Alley" area of the central U.S. to guide groups of people who want to witness the brute force of tornadoes in action

programme.title: Angry Planet: Island Caving
programme.desc: Accompanied by a group of hardcore cavers from Alberta and British Columbia, George descends into spectacular caves that lead deep into the earth on Vancouver Island. The team carries inflatable kayaks to explore a dark underground river.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Polynesian Volcanoes
programme.desc: In New Zealand, George learns that an undersea volcano 1000 miles North in Tonga is creating a new island. After nautical mishaps, clad in nothing but underwear and a life jacket, George becomes one of the first explorers on this new land.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Desert Monsoon
programme.desc: Enlisting the help of fellow storm chaser R.J. Evans, George heads to Arizona to photograph spectacular lightning storms triggered by the desert monsoon season. R.J. however is known as "America's noisiest athiest" for his free-thought radio show.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Thunder Down Under
programme.desc: In Australia George teams up with storm chaser Jimmy Deguara to witness summer storms in the outback. While in Oz, he dives the magnificent Great Barrier Reef, and explores just how painful is the sting of the venomous Box Jellyfish.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact
programme.desc: Throughout history, the earth has been bombarded from space. Asteroid impacts have caused mass extinctions – including the dinosaur wipe out.

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of Tornado
programme.desc: Tornados are one of the most destructive forces of the planets with winds at 300 miles an hour. They can destroy buildings and hurl cars hundreds of feet through the air.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Great Flood
programme.desc: Almost every culture on Earth tells the story of an ancient flood – details vary but the basic plot is the same - An angry God wipes out an unrepentant population.

programme.title: Naked Science: What's Wrong With Pluto
programme.desc: Naked Science investigates the violent cosmological forces that create planets and discover what it is that makes a planet a planet.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Wildfires
programme.desc: With flames roaring to heights of 50 meters or more and temperatures exceeding 1,200°C, the wildfire is one of Mother Nature’s most horrific killers. Wildfires show no signs of stopping and their inexorability on this planet is truly terrifying.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Blizzards
programme.desc: With ice, freezing temperatures, and ferocious winds — the Blizzard is one of Mother Nature’s most effective killing machines. They are typically associated with heavy snow, but may even occur when there is little or no snowfall.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Floods
programme.desc: Even when below your knees, you can still be swept off your feet by the torrent of water. And the danger doesn’t stop there: electric shocks, contaminated water and washed away vehicles are perilous.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Pandemics
programme.desc: With an ever more mobile world population, potentially growing in size to 11 billion people by the next century, public health experts agree that the possibility of a cataclysmic disease is more likely now than ever before.

programme.title: Deadly Disasters: Hurricanes
programme.desc: With a combination of torrential rain, powerful winds, and devastating storm surges, Hurricanes are among Mother Nature’s most calamitous natural disasters.

programme.title: Rugrats in Paris: The Movie
programme.desc: When Stu Pickles is summoned to work at EuroReptarland, Paris’ newest amusement park, the Rugrats kids tag along. From the Eiffel Tower to Notre Dame, no attraction is safe from the Rugrats kids who find that fun is the same in any language.

programme.title: Rugrats Go Wild
programme.desc: The Rugrats family vacation takes an exotic detour when their boat capsizes and they become shipwrecked on a deserted tropical island.

programme.title: Chop Kick Panda
programme.desc: A lazy panda named Zibo dreams of becoming a martial arts master but is stuck as a cleaner for a dojo. Little does he know that an evil tiger has plans to take over the kingdom and only Zibo can stop him in this fun adventure!

programme.title: The Secret of N.I.M.H. 2: Timmy to the Rescue
programme.desc: The rats and mice, made intellectually superior in the original Secret of N.I.M.H., return to Thorn Valley to groom their destined leader - young Timmy Brisby.

programme.title: Sherlock Gnomes
programme.desc: Join a phe-gnome-nal adventure inside the secret world of garden gnomes! When Gnomeo and Juliet discover their friends are missing, they team up with detective SHERLOCK GNOMES to solve the mystery and bring the gnomes home in this family comedy.

programme.title: The Little Prince
programme.desc: A pilot crash-lands in the Sahara and finds a boy who says he's a prince fallen to Earth from his asteroid home.

programme.title: Norm of the North
programme.desc: In this animated family adventure Norm the polar bear travels to New York City after having to leave his home in the arctic, along with his three lemming friends.

programme.title: Norm of the North 2: Key to the Kingdom
programme.desc: Norm of the North is back! With the help of his friends, family, and some new characters, the king of the Arctic must save New York City and his home.

programme.title: Norm of the North: Family Vacation
programme.desc: Overwhelmed by his Kingly duties, Norm struggles to make time for both his kingdom and his family. But when his crown is mysteriously stolen in the dead of the night, Norm must embark on an epic journey — disguised as a family vacation.

programme.title: Norm of the North
programme.desc: In this animated family adventure Norm the polar bear travels to New York City after having to leave his home in the arctic, along with his three lemming friends.

programme.title: Norm of the North 2: Key to the Kingdom
programme.desc: Norm of the North is back! With the help of his friends, family, and some new characters, the king of the Arctic must save New York City and his home.

programme.title: Wonder Park
programme.desc: Buckle up for a fun-tastic family adventure where anything is possible! Future inventor June discovers her dream amusement park is real. But when trouble hits, she takes her misfit team of animal friends on an unforgettable journey to save the park.

programme.title: Sherlock Gnomes
programme.desc: Join a phe-gnome-nal adventure inside the secret world of garden gnomes! When Gnomeo and Juliet discover their friends are missing, they team up with detective SHERLOCK GNOMES to solve the mystery and bring the gnomes home in this family comedy.

programme.title: The Little Prince
programme.desc: A pilot crash-lands in the Sahara and finds a boy who says he's a prince fallen to Earth from his asteroid home.

programme.title: Sherlock Gnomes
programme.desc: Join a phe-gnome-nal adventure inside the secret world of garden gnomes! When Gnomeo and Juliet discover their friends are missing, they team up with detective SHERLOCK GNOMES to solve the mystery and bring the gnomes home in this family comedy.

programme.title: Wonder Park
programme.desc: Buckle up for a fun-tastic family adventure where anything is possible! Future inventor June discovers her dream amusement park is real. But when trouble hits, she takes her misfit team of animal friends on an unforgettable journey to save the park.

programme.title: The Little Prince
programme.desc: A pilot crash-lands in the Sahara and finds a boy who says he's a prince fallen to Earth from his asteroid home.

programme.title: Barnyard
programme.desc: Wild, wacky and “udderly” hilarious, here’s a herd of animated pranksters that’ll keep you laughing out loud!

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: Universe or Multiverse?
programme.desc: Cutting-edge theories are suggesting that our universe may not be the only universe. Instead, it may be just one of an infinite number of worlds that make up the multiverse.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: What Is Space?
programme.desc: Brian Greene reveals space as a dynamic fabric that can stretch, twist, warp, and ripple under the influence of gravity, and explores dark energy, which makes up 70% of the universe.

programme.title: The Fabric of the Cosmos: The Illusion of Time
programme.desc: Brian Greene takes us on the ultimate time traveling adventure, hurtling 50 years into the future before stepping into a wormhole to travel back to Big Bang.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Flight Status
programme.desc: The latest updates on the new space hardware about to launch us into the next generation of space flight some rockets and capsules are on time others not so fortunate.

programme.title: The New Frontier: PEERING INTO THE ABYSS
programme.desc: Imaging a black hole was a breakthrough in astronomy, now with the new satellite IXPE high energy telescope we will be looking at some of the most dramatic, and most violent objects in the universe: Colliding Black holes and Neutron Stars.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Gravity
programme.desc: LISA pathfinder, has led the way for a space based gravity wave detector, what are gravity waves and why are they so hard to detect?

programme.title: The New Frontier: Set Course For Heart of the Sun
programme.desc: NASA’S Parker Solar Probe Mission is on its way and will be shortly followed by Europe’s Solar probe Mission.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Proving Einstein
programme.desc: Some of the proof of Einstein’s theories had long been elusive, beyond the capabilities of technology, until now.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Sun
programme.desc: It powers the machinery of nature; our weather, encourages and sustains life on land and at sea.

programme.title: The New Frontier: From There To Here
programme.desc: Series introduction and the study of Earth from orbit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Interface
programme.desc: The Ionisphere, where the Earth interfaces with space, is little understood, it effects radio communications, GPS signals and satellites alike.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Where Are All the Aliens?
programme.desc: We’re looking hard, but the numbers aren’t stacking up, is life on other planets that hard to find? Even signals from an advanced race elude us.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Exo Planeta
programme.desc: As scientists and astronomers peer closer at nearby stars with ever more fidelity they are discovering planets; exo planets, worlds outside our own solar system.

programme.title: Moon Shots: Inside the Lost Apollo Archive
programme.desc: The world famous Apollo missions are some of the greatest feats ever accomplished by man. With rare archive footage and unprecedented access to life on board the Apollo spacecrafts, this stunning one-hour special brings scientific history to life.

programme.title: How to Colonize the Stars
programme.desc: Leading physicists and engineers in the field of interstellar travel discuss some of the deep issues we face when considering the problem of sending spacecraft to other stars. Will mankind ever truly become a space fairing civilization?

programme.title: Earthrise: The First Lunar Voyage
programme.desc: Interviews with Apollo 8 astronauts, their wives, mission control staff, and journalists give true insider's perspective to the space race of the late 1960's.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Tickets Please
programme.desc: No spacecraft have traveled so fast or so close to a star before. It is hoped to reveal the source of the solar winds that effect us here on Earth

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Astronauts
programme.desc: One hundred kilometres above the Earth’s surface lies an invisible frontier: the edge of space.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Hyabusa
programme.desc: The Japanese Asteroid sample return mission has reached its target and is surveying the space rock ready to dip down and take a sample.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Heavy Lift
programme.desc: New heavy lift rockets are taking to the sky. Falcon Heavy, SLS, Arianne 6 and Vega C rockets are primed to begin the next phase of space expansion.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Living Planet
programme.desc: With continuous observation of arth from orbit scientists have built a data set over time that reveals much about how the Earth breaths and life sustains itself on this living Planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Planet X
programme.desc: The New Horizons mission continues deeper into space, it’s next target looming close.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Inner Worlds
programme.desc: They orbit between us and our Star, the inner worlds; hidden within the sun's glare often only visible at sunrise or sunset.

programme.title: EARTH´S EVIL TWIN
programme.desc: Venus is a planet closest to Earth in distance and size, it once may have been as hospitable to life as Earth is now. Then something changed and Venus became shrouded in hot dense toxic clouds.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mercury Or Bust
programme.desc: The ESA - JAXA joint Bepi-Colombo Mission is moments away from launch.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Edge of The Universe
programme.desc: The night sky is a time machine. The further we look out into the universe the further back in time we reach.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Milky Way
programme.desc: This episode traces our view of the Milky Way, from the mythology of ancient sky watchers to the efforts of scientists to map it from the inside out.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Water Worlds
programme.desc: The key ingredient to life in the universe is water. Until recently we thought Earth was the only place in the solar system to have any water at all. Now that probes are peering around nearby worlds we are finding that is just not the case.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: The Big Bang
programme.desc: Throughout history, our nightly view of the universe has fueled our collective imagination. When it comes to the Big Bang, not even the most creative work of imagination can rival what science has revealed about the birth of the universe.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas
programme.desc: See the sun, moon, planets, and galaxies through the eyes of intrepid machines that are blazing a trail across the solar system. A gateway to bold adventures, these cameras are delivering spectacular new cosmic vistas.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas
programme.desc: See the sun, moon, planets, and galaxies through the eyes of intrepid machines that are blazing a trail across the solar system. A gateway to bold adventures, these cameras are delivering spectacular new cosmic vistas.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Hubble's Renaissance
programme.desc: Before we can reach space, we must first be able to see it. Hundreds of years ago, the simple telescope was used to give us a seemingly closer vantage. Today, we use the awe-inspiring, and super powerful, Hubble Space Telescope.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Gravity
programme.desc: Gravity has long been a force that has intrigued scientists. Now, for the first time scientists are probing to detect gravity waves in space as well as mapping the gravity patterns of the moon.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Night Fire
programme.desc: The Northern Lights are more than just beautiful; they tell us about the make up of our planet, our sun, and may even be a signpost for the possibility of life on other planets.

programme.title: Hubble's Canvas: Colour
programme.desc: Every Hubble image begins in black and white, so why are Hubble’s pictures so colourful? The telescope has a wide range of filters that can be assigned different colours.

programme.title: Hubble's Canvas: Cosmic Perspective
programme.desc: Our ancestors thought the sky was flat, but we now know it as a place of infinite depth.

programme.title: Hubble's Canvas: Grand Design
programme.desc: The forces of nature, operating at large and small scales create a universe of symmetry and form.

programme.title: Hubble's Canvas: Vanishing Point
programme.desc: There are monsters loose in the cosmos, giant black holes that devour matter and spit out high-speed jets of atoms.

programme.title: Hubble's Canvas: Eye of the Beholder
programme.desc: Hubble was created to dispense with the biggest problem in astronomy, the interference caused by earth’s atmosphere.

programme.title: Hubble's Canvas: Painted by Light
programme.desc: The universe paints pictures with light, so to understand what a Hubble picture can reveal about the universe, it’s necessary to understand how light is emitted, absorbed and reflected by stars, gas and dust.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Dark Side
programme.desc: Perhaps that American politician was right. There are indeed things we know we don’t know.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Stars Above
programme.desc: Stars are a bit like human beings. They can be warm or cold, they come in all kinds of shapes and sizes.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Cosmic Collisions
programme.desc: Cosmic and x rays, these mysterious and highly powerful particles from deep space are being investigated to finally discover where they come from.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Invasion on Mars
programme.desc: The EXO Mars and Curiosity continue their scientific discoveries as we prepare new missions both robotic and manned to the Red planet Mars.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mars: Adapt Or Leave
programme.desc: As the latest successes and failures come and go Mars continues to give up her secrets.

programme.title: STAR LIGHT, STAR BRIGHT
programme.desc: The two current science missions to our star; the sun have made great leaps forward in the science of how stars work, how they generate solar winds and how they affect their orbiting planets

programme.title: MOON SHINE
programme.desc: The International drive to return to the Moon is gathering pace, this multi- nation effort is readying to return, stay and utilise the Lunar assets.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Next Stop Mars
programme.desc: It has fired our imaginations for millennia, Mars, the God of War and the source of Man's science fictional demise.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Juno, Cassini and Beyond
programme.desc: The Cassini space craft has completed its mission over Saturn, Juno has reached its target and has commenced its scientific observations of the giant planet Jupiter.

programme.title: The New Frontier: INCREASING ORBIT
programme.desc: The ISS is the workhorse of the international spacefaring community, but its days are numbered. R

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Landsat
programme.desc: Take a look at Landsat 8, the advanced planetary explorer with a 900 million dollar price tag! It is designed to peer across multiple wavelengths to reveal complex and ever-changing details that are undetectable by the human eye.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Stargazer's Paradise
programme.desc: The Atacama desert is a perfect place for watching the night sky. With still winds and only a handful of cloudy nights each year, it allows scientists to get the absolute most out of their tools.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Vesta Unveiled
programme.desc: With the Dawn missions exploration of the asteroid Vesta and the dwarf planet Ceres, asteroids are a hot topic. See what these incredible missions have uncovered.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Landing on Mars
programme.desc: Mars sparks our curiosity like no other planet because it is so reachable. Despite having landed there, most of the information we gather is still done by instruments in the planet's orbit.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas
programme.desc: See the sun, moon, planets, and galaxies through the eyes of intrepid machines that are blazing a trail across the solar system. A gateway to bold adventures, these cameras are delivering spectacular new cosmic vistas.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 13
programme.desc: Teams travel to Sydney where Russian friends Olga & Valeria are determined to put the food of their motherland on the map.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 14
programme.desc: Teams travel to Australia’s home of big things, Coffs Harbour, where sisters Georgie & Alicia are planning to add a big score to the list

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 15
programme.desc: Teams are deployed to Brisbane where defence force foodies, Matt & Aly, are gearing up to impress the troops.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 16
programme.desc: Teams travel to Sydney where mum and daughter, Pat & Louisa, are channeling their Hungarian gypsy roots to deliver a show-stopping menu.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 17

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 18

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 19
programme.desc: It’s Group 1 vs Group 2 in a true blue elimination challenge. The competition is turned on its head and new rivalries are forged when an epic first challenge sees the weakest team bring their whole group crashing down.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 20
programme.desc: Group 1 teams are on their best behaviour for the first location challenge -a high tea service where they’ll be judged by the public and, for the first time on MKR, their rivals.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 21

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 22
programme.desc: The weakest teams from each Group Challenge meet at Elimination House where they face the biggest table of judges in MKR history

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 24
programme.desc: Group 1 is set for some friendly competition as they celebrate two fun Australian pastimes –lawn bowls and a great BBQ! Which team will bowl ‘em over and who will see red after a day on the green?

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 25
programme.desc: The weakest teams from each Group Challenge meet at Elimination House.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 26

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 27
programme.desc: Group 2 is bringing the heat to Spice Alley, rocking the woks at their very own Asian street food stalls

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 28
programme.desc: The weakest teams from each Group Challenge meet at Elimination House. One team will achieve perfection while the other will be sent packing in a heartbreaking sudden death cook-off.

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 29

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 30

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 31

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 32

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 34

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 35

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 36

programme.title: My Kitchen Rules Australia: Episode 37
